artifact_text:
# statistics.py

A zero-dependency module providing exact-arithmetic measures of central tendency and spread for numeric data.

## Public functions

**`mean(data)`** — Returns the sample arithmetic mean of `data`; raises `StatisticsError` if `data` is empty. Preserves the numeric type of the input (int, float, Fraction, Decimal).

**`harmonic_mean(data)`** — Returns the harmonic mean (reciprocal of the mean of reciprocals) of `data`; appropriate for averaging rates or ratios. Raises `StatisticsError` for empty data or negative values; returns 0 if any element is zero.

**`median(data)`** — Returns the median of numeric `data`. For an odd number of points, returns the middle value; for an even number, returns the arithmetic mean of the two middle values as a float. Raises `StatisticsError` for empty data.

**`median_low(data)`** — Returns the low median of `data`. Always returns an actual data point: the middle value for odd-length data, or the lower of the two middle values for even-length data. Raises `StatisticsError` for empty data.

**`median_high(data)`** — Returns the high median of `data`. Always returns an actual data point: the middle value for odd-length data, or the upper of the two middle values for even-length data. Raises `StatisticsError` for empty data.

**`median_grouped(data, interval=1)`** — Returns the 50th percentile of grouped continuous data using linear interpolation within the median class interval. The optional `interval` argument specifies the class width (default 1). Raises `StatisticsError` for empty data.

**`mode(data)`** — Returns the single most common value from discrete or nominal data. Raises `StatisticsError` if the data is empty or if there is no unique mode (i.e., two or more values tie for highest frequency).

**`variance(data, xbar=None)`** — Returns the sample variance of `data` (denominator n−1); requires at least two data points. If the mean has already been computed it may be passed as `xbar` to avoid a second pass. Raises `StatisticsError` for fewer than two points.

**`pvariance(data, mu=None)`** — Returns the population variance of `data` (denominator n); requires at least one data point. If the mean has already been computed it may be passed as `mu`. Raises `StatisticsError` for empty data.

**`stdev(data, xbar=None)`** — Returns the sample standard deviation; the square root of `variance(data, xbar)`. Accepts the same arguments and raises the same errors as `variance`.

**`pstdev(data, mu=None)`** — Returns the population standard deviation; the square root of `pvariance(data, mu)`. Accepts the same arguments and raises the same errors as `pvariance`.

**`StatisticsError`** — Subclass of `ValueError` raised for invalid inputs such as empty data, insufficient data points, or a non-unique mode.

## How the pieces fit together

The averaging functions (`mean`, `harmonic_mean`) and the spread functions (`variance`, `pvariance`, `stdev`, `pstdev`) all route through the internal `_sum` helper, which accumulates totals as exact `Fraction` values to avoid floating-point cancellation error, then converts back to the dominant input type; the median and mode functions sort or count their inputs directly and do not rely on `_sum`.

artifact_text:
# statistics.py

A numeric statistics library providing measures of central tendency and spread for real-valued data, supporting int, float, Fraction, and Decimal types.

## Public Functions

**mean(data)** — Returns the arithmetic mean (sum divided by count); raises `StatisticsError` on empty input.

**harmonic_mean(data)** — Returns the harmonic mean (reciprocal of the mean of reciprocals); appropriate for rates and ratios; raises `StatisticsError` on empty or negative input.

**median(data)** — Returns the middle value of sorted data; interpolates the average of the two middle values when the count is even.

**median_low(data)** — Returns the median, always choosing the lower of the two middle values when the count is even.

**median_high(data)** — Returns the median, always choosing the upper of the two middle values when the count is even.

**median_grouped(data, interval=1)** — Returns the 50th percentile of grouped continuous data using interpolation within a class interval; `interval` controls the assumed class width.

**mode(data)** — Returns the single most common value; raises `StatisticsError` if there is no unique mode or the data is empty.

**variance(data, xbar=None)** — Returns the sample variance (divided by n−1); optionally accepts a pre-computed mean to skip recalculation.

**pvariance(data, mu=None)** — Returns the population variance (divided by n); optionally accepts a pre-computed mean.

**stdev(data, xbar=None)** — Returns the square root of the sample variance; delegates to `variance`.

**pstdev(data, mu=None)** — Returns the square root of the population variance; delegates to `pvariance`.

## How the pieces fit together

The averaging functions (`mean`, `harmonic_mean`) and spread functions (`variance`, `pvariance`, `stdev`, `pstdev`) share a high-precision internal summation pipeline (`_sum`, `_exact_ratio`, `_coerce`, `_convert`) that avoids floating-point round-off by accumulating results as `Fraction` values before converting back to the input's numeric type; the median variants and `mode` are independent sort- and count-based calculations that use `StatisticsError` (a `ValueError` subclass) as the single shared exception.

artifact_text:
# statistics.py — Module Notes

## Provenance

Vendored unmodified from CPython v3.7.9 (`Lib/statistics.py`). See PINNED.txt for the
upstream commit and license details (PSF License Agreement).

## Purpose

Provides pure-Python descriptive statistics functions operating on sequences of real
numbers. No third-party dependencies; relies only on stdlib (`math`, `fractions`,
`decimal`, `collections`, `bisect`, `itertools`).

## Public API

### Averages

| Function | Description |
|---|---|
| `mean(data)` | Arithmetic mean. Requires ≥1 data point. |
| `harmonic_mean(data)` | Harmonic mean. Requires ≥1 point; rejects negative values. |
| `median(data)` | Middle value; interpolates (average of two middle values) for even-length data. |
| `median_low(data)` | Always returns an actual data point; picks the lower middle for even-length data. |
| `median_high(data)` | Always returns an actual data point; picks the upper middle for even-length data. |
| `median_grouped(data, interval=1)` | 50th percentile by linear interpolation for grouped/continuous data. |
| `mode(data)` | Most common value. Raises `StatisticsError` if no unique mode. Works on non-numeric data. |

### Spread

| Function | Description |
|---|---|
| `variance(data, xbar=None)` | Sample variance (divides by n−1). Requires ≥2 points. |
| `pvariance(data, mu=None)` | Population variance (divides by n). Requires ≥1 point. |
| `stdev(data, xbar=None)` | Sample standard deviation (square root of `variance`). |
| `pstdev(data, mu=None)` | Population standard deviation (square root of `pvariance`). |

All four spread functions accept an optional pre-computed mean to avoid a redundant pass
over the data.

### Exception

`StatisticsError(ValueError)` — raised for empty data, insufficient data points, or
ambiguous results (e.g. non-unique mode).

## Key Implementation Notes

**High-precision summation (`_sum`).** Rather than Python's built-in `sum`, the module
accumulates values as `Fraction` objects grouped by denominator. This avoids the
floating-point round-off errors that plague the naive "computational formula" for
variance. The result is then converted back to the input numeric type.

**Type coercion.** Mixed numeric types are rejected except that `int` is silently
promoted to the other type. The coercion ladder is: `int` → `Fraction` → `float` →
`Decimal` (subclasses handled via `issubclass` checks in `_coerce`).

**Two-pass variance (`_ss`).** Variance is computed with two passes over the data — one
for the mean, one for squared deviations — and applies a second-order correction term
(`total2**2/n`) to reduce rounding error. The "computational formula" (single-pass) is
explicitly avoided; see comments at `statistics.py:514–523`.

**Grouped median interpolation.** `median_grouped` uses bisection (`bisect_left` /
`bisect_right`) to locate class boundaries in O(log n) rather than a linear scan, then
applies the standard interpolation formula: `L + interval * (n/2 − cf) / f`.

artifact_text:
A module providing basic numeric statistics functions — averages, medians, mode, variance, and standard deviation — with exact-arithmetic support for `int`, `float`, `Fraction`, and `Decimal` types.

## Public API

**`StatisticsError`** — Exception raised for invalid inputs (e.g., empty data, no unique mode); subclass of `ValueError`.

**`mean(data)`** — Returns the arithmetic mean of `data` using high-precision fractional summation; preserves the numeric type of the input.

**`harmonic_mean(data)`** — Returns the harmonic mean (reciprocal of the mean of reciprocals); appropriate for rates and ratios; raises `StatisticsError` on negative values or empty input.

**`median(data)`** — Returns the median of `data`; interpolates the average of the two middle values when the count is even.

**`median_low(data)`** — Returns the low median; when the count is even, returns the smaller of the two middle values rather than interpolating.

**`median_high(data)`** — Returns the high median; when the count is even, returns the larger of the two middle values rather than interpolating.

**`median_grouped(data, interval=1)`** — Returns the 50th percentile of grouped continuous data using interpolation within a class interval; `interval` controls the assumed class width.

**`mode(data)`** — Returns the single most common value in `data`; works with any hashable type; raises `StatisticsError` if there is no unique mode or the data is empty.

**`variance(data, xbar=None)`** — Returns the sample variance (denominator `n-1`); accepts a precomputed mean as `xbar` to avoid a second pass; requires at least two data points.

**`pvariance(data, mu=None)`** — Returns the population variance (denominator `n`); accepts a precomputed mean as `mu`; requires at least one data point.

**`stdev(data, xbar=None)`** — Returns the sample standard deviation; square root of `variance(data, xbar)`.

**`pstdev(data, mu=None)`** — Returns the population standard deviation; square root of `pvariance(data, mu)`.

All spread functions share a two-pass algorithm (mean first, then deviations) to avoid the numerical instability of the one-pass computational formula, and all averages and spread functions delegate exact summation to the same internal `_sum` helper so that type-preservation and precision are handled consistently throughout the module.

artifact_text:
# statistics.py

A numeric statistics library providing measures of central tendency and spread for real-valued data, supporting int, float, Fraction, and Decimal types.

## Public functions

**mean** — Returns the arithmetic mean (sum divided by count) of the data; raises `StatisticsError` if data is empty.

**harmonic_mean** — Returns the harmonic mean (reciprocal of the mean of reciprocals) of the data; useful for averaging rates or ratios; raises `StatisticsError` for empty data or negative values.

**median** — Returns the middle value of sorted data; for even-length data, returns the average of the two middle values.

**median_low** — Returns the median always choosing an actual data point; for even-length data, returns the lower of the two middle values.

**median_high** — Returns the median always choosing an actual data point; for even-length data, returns the higher of the two middle values.

**median_grouped** — Returns the 50th percentile of grouped continuous data using interpolation within a class interval (default width 1); intended for data that has been rounded or binned.

**mode** — Returns the single most common value in the data; raises `StatisticsError` if the data is empty or if there is no unique mode.

**variance** — Returns the sample variance (sum of squared deviations divided by n−1); requires at least two data points; accepts an optional pre-computed mean `xbar` to avoid recalculation.

**pvariance** — Returns the population variance (sum of squared deviations divided by n); requires at least one data point; accepts an optional pre-computed mean `mu`.

**stdev** — Returns the sample standard deviation (square root of `variance`).

**pstdev** — Returns the population standard deviation (square root of `pvariance`).

The averaging functions feed into the spread functions: `stdev`/`pstdev` delegate to `variance`/`pvariance`, which in turn use `mean` internally, allowing callers to short-circuit mean computation by passing it as an optional argument.

artifact_text:
# statistics.py

A small, zero-dependency library of descriptive statistics functions covering central tendency and spread, with exact-arithmetic support for `int`, `float`, `Fraction`, and `Decimal` inputs.

## Public functions

**`mean(data)`** — Returns the sample arithmetic mean of the data; raises `StatisticsError` if `data` is empty.

**`harmonic_mean(data)`** — Returns the harmonic mean (reciprocal of the mean of reciprocals); raises `StatisticsError` on empty input or negative values, and returns 0 if any value is zero.

**`median(data)`** — Returns the median of numeric data; for an even number of points, interpolates by averaging the two middle values. Raises `StatisticsError` on empty input.

**`median_low(data)`** — Returns the low median; for an even number of points, returns the smaller of the two middle values rather than interpolating.

**`median_high(data)`** — Returns the high median; for an even number of points, returns the larger of the two middle values rather than interpolating.

**`median_grouped(data, interval=1)`** — Returns the 50th percentile of grouped continuous data using linear interpolation within the median class interval; `interval` sets the assumed class width.

**`mode(data)`** — Returns the single most common value from discrete or nominal data; raises `StatisticsError` if the data is empty or if there is no unique mode (i.e., two or more values tie for most frequent).

**`variance(data, xbar=None)`** — Returns the sample variance (denominator `n − 1`); requires at least two data points. Pass a previously computed mean as `xbar` to avoid recalculating it.

**`pvariance(data, mu=None)`** — Returns the population variance (denominator `n`); requires at least one data point. Pass a previously computed mean as `mu` to avoid recalculating it.

**`stdev(data, xbar=None)`** — Returns the sample standard deviation; the square root of `variance(data, xbar)`.

**`pstdev(data, mu=None)`** — Returns the population standard deviation; the square root of `pvariance(data, mu)`.

**`StatisticsError`** — A `ValueError` subclass raised for invalid inputs such as empty data, non-unique modes, or negative values where they are not permitted.

## How the pieces fit together

The averaging functions (`mean`, `harmonic_mean`) and the spread functions (`variance`, `pvariance`, `stdev`, `pstdev`) all route through a shared high-precision summation path (`_sum` → `_exact_ratio` → `Fraction` arithmetic) so that exact-arithmetic types are handled consistently and floating-point rounding is minimised across all public entry points.

artifact_text:
# NOTES: statistics.py

A numeric statistics library providing measures of central tendency and spread for real-valued data, with high-precision arithmetic via `Fraction` internals.

## Public functions

**mean** — Returns the arithmetic mean (sum divided by count) of a non-empty dataset; supports int, float, Fraction, and Decimal.

**harmonic_mean** — Returns the harmonic mean (reciprocal of the mean of reciprocals) of a non-empty, non-negative dataset; suited for averaging rates or ratios.

**median** — Returns the middle value of sorted data; interpolates the average of the two middle values when the count is even.

**median_low** — Returns the median, always choosing the lower of the two middle values when the count is even (never interpolates).

**median_high** — Returns the median, always choosing the higher of the two middle values when the count is even (never interpolates).

**median_grouped** — Returns the 50th percentile of grouped continuous data using linear interpolation within the median class interval; accepts an optional `interval` argument (default 1).

**mode** — Returns the single most common value in discrete or nominal data; raises `StatisticsError` if there is no unique mode.

**variance** — Returns the sample variance (divides by n−1) of at least two data points; accepts an optional pre-computed mean `xbar` to avoid a redundant pass.

**pvariance** — Returns the population variance (divides by n) of at least one data point; accepts an optional pre-computed mean `mu`.

**stdev** — Returns the sample standard deviation as the square root of `variance`.

**pstdev** — Returns the population standard deviation as the square root of `pvariance`.

## How the pieces fit together

The averages (`mean`, `harmonic_mean`, `median*`, `mode`) and spread functions (`variance`, `pvariance`, `stdev`, `pstdev`) are independent entry points that share a common high-precision summation core (`_sum`, `_ss`), ensuring numeric stability across all supported numeric types.

artifact_text:
# statistics.py

A vendored numeric statistics library providing measures of central tendency and spread for real-valued data. Supports `int`, `float`, `Fraction`, and `Decimal` inputs; the return type matches the input type.

## Public API

### Averages

| Function | Description |
|---|---|
| `mean(data)` | Arithmetic mean. Requires ≥1 value. |
| `harmonic_mean(data)` | Harmonic mean. Requires ≥1 value; rejects negatives. Appropriate for rates/ratios. |
| `median(data)` | Middle value; interpolates average of two middle values for even-length data. |
| `median_low(data)` | Always returns an actual data point; picks the lower of the two middle values. |
| `median_high(data)` | Always returns an actual data point; picks the higher of the two middle values. |
| `median_grouped(data, interval=1)` | 50th percentile for grouped continuous data via interpolation. |
| `mode(data)` | Most common value. Raises `StatisticsError` if there is no unique mode. Works with non-numeric data. |

### Spread

| Function | Description |
|---|---|
| `variance(data, xbar=None)` | Sample variance (divides by n−1). Requires ≥2 values. |
| `pvariance(data, mu=None)` | Population variance (divides by n). Requires ≥1 value. |
| `stdev(data, xbar=None)` | Square root of `variance`. |
| `pstdev(data, mu=None)` | Square root of `pvariance`. |

The optional `xbar`/`mu` argument lets you pass a pre-computed mean to avoid recalculating it.

## Exceptions

`StatisticsError(ValueError)` — raised for empty data, insufficient data points, negative values where not allowed, or a non-unique mode.

## Implementation notes

- Summation uses `_sum()`, which accumulates via `Fraction` internally to avoid floating-point round-off. This is more precise than Python's built-in `sum()`.
- Variance is computed using a two-pass algorithm (mean first, then deviations) rather than the "computational formula", which is numerically unstable.
- `median_grouped` uses bisection search (`bisect_left`/`bisect_right`) for O(log n) lookup of class boundaries.

A module providing basic descriptive statistics — averages, medians, mode, variance, and standard deviation — with exact-arithmetic support for `int`, `float`, `Fraction`, and `Decimal` types.

## Public functions

**`mean(data)`** — Returns the arithmetic mean of *data*; raises `StatisticsError` if *data* is empty.

**`harmonic_mean(data)`** — Returns the harmonic mean (reciprocal of the mean of reciprocals) of *data*; appropriate for averaging rates and ratios; raises `StatisticsError` for empty input or negative values.

**`median(data)`** — Returns the median of *data*; interpolates the average of the two middle values when the count is even; raises `StatisticsError` if *data* is empty.

**`median_low(data)`** — Returns the low median of *data*: the middle value for odd-length data, or the smaller of the two middle values for even-length data; raises `StatisticsError` if *data* is empty.

**`median_high(data)`** — Returns the high median of *data*: the middle value for odd-length data, or the larger of the two middle values for even-length data; raises `StatisticsError` if *data* is empty.

**`median_grouped(data, interval=1)`** — Returns the 50th percentile of grouped continuous data by interpolation within the median class interval; accepts an optional `interval` argument (default 1) representing the class width; raises `StatisticsError` if *data* is empty.

**`mode(data)`** — Returns the single most common value in *data*; works with any hashable type, including non-numeric data; raises `StatisticsError` if *data* is empty or if there is no unique mode.

**`variance(data, xbar=None)`** — Returns the sample variance of *data* (divides by *n*−1); requires at least two data points; accepts a precomputed mean as `xbar` to avoid a redundant pass.

**`pvariance(data, mu=None)`** — Returns the population variance of *data* (divides by *n*); requires at least one data point; accepts a precomputed mean as `mu`.

**`stdev(data, xbar=None)`** — Returns the sample standard deviation (square root of `variance`); accepts the same arguments as `variance`.

**`pstdev(data, mu=None)`** — Returns the population standard deviation (square root of `pvariance`); accepts the same arguments as `pvariance`.

**`StatisticsError`** — Exception class (subclass of `ValueError`) raised for invalid inputs such as empty data or a non-unique mode.

## How the pieces fit together

The averaging and spread functions share an exact-arithmetic core (`_sum`, `_exact_ratio`, `_coerce`, `_convert`) that accumulates sums as `Fraction` values and converts back to the appropriate numeric type on output, preserving precision for `Fraction` and `Decimal` inputs while handling the common `float` and `int` cases efficiently.

A numeric statistics library providing averages, variance, and standard deviation for real-valued data.

**mean** — Returns the arithmetic mean of a non-empty dataset; supports int, float, Fraction, and Decimal.

**harmonic_mean** — Returns the harmonic mean (reciprocal of the mean of reciprocals); appropriate for rates and ratios; rejects negative values.

**median** — Returns the middle value of sorted data; interpolates the average of the two middle values when the count is even.

**median_low** — Returns the lower of the two middle values when the count is even, otherwise the middle value.

**median_high** — Returns the higher of the two middle values when the count is even, otherwise the middle value.

**median_grouped** — Returns the 50th percentile of grouped continuous data using interpolation within a class interval; accepts an optional `interval` argument (default 1).

**mode** — Returns the single most common value in discrete or nominal data; raises `StatisticsError` if there is no unique mode.

**variance** — Returns the sample variance (divides by n−1); accepts an optional precomputed mean `xbar` to avoid recalculation.

**pvariance** — Returns the population variance (divides by n); accepts an optional precomputed mean `mu`.

**stdev** — Returns the square root of the sample variance; thin wrapper around `variance`.

**pstdev** — Returns the square root of the population variance; thin wrapper around `pvariance`.

The averaging functions feed into the spread functions: `variance` and `pvariance` internally call `mean` (unless a precomputed mean is supplied), and `stdev`/`pstdev` delegate directly to their variance counterparts.

artifact_text:
# statistics.py

Vendored from CPython v3.7.9 (`Lib/statistics.py`), licensed under the PSF License Agreement.

## Purpose

Provides pure-Python functions for computing descriptive statistics of numeric data. No external dependencies — uses only the standard library (`collections`, `math`, `numbers`, `fractions`, `decimal`, `itertools`, `bisect`).

## Public API

### Averages

| Function | Description |
|---|---|
| `mean(data)` | Arithmetic mean. Requires at least one value. |
| `harmonic_mean(data)` | Harmonic mean. Useful for rates/ratios; rejects negative values. |
| `median(data)` | Standard median; interpolates between the two middle values for even-length data. |
| `median_low(data)` | Always returns an actual data point; picks the lower middle for even-length data. |
| `median_high(data)` | Always returns an actual data point; picks the upper middle for even-length data. |
| `median_grouped(data, interval=1)` | 50th percentile via interpolation, for grouped/continuous data. |
| `mode(data)` | Most common value; raises `StatisticsError` if there is no unique mode. |

### Spread

| Function | Description |
|---|---|
| `variance(data, xbar=None)` | Sample variance (divides by n−1). Requires at least two values. |
| `pvariance(data, mu=None)` | Population variance (divides by n). Requires at least one value. |
| `stdev(data, xbar=None)` | Square root of `variance`. |
| `pstdev(data, mu=None)` | Square root of `pvariance`. |

The optional `xbar`/`mu` argument lets callers supply a previously computed mean to avoid a second pass over the data.

### Exception

`StatisticsError(ValueError)` — raised for empty data, insufficient data points, no unique mode, or negative values where they are disallowed.

## Numeric precision

The module avoids the floating-point pitfalls of the naive computational formula for variance. Internally it uses `_sum()`, which accumulates results as exact `Fraction` values (partitioned by denominator) before converting back to the input type. This preserves precision for `float`, `Fraction`, and `Decimal` inputs. Mixed numeric types are rejected unless one of them is `int`.

artifact_text:
# statistics.py

A small, dependency-free library of descriptive statistics functions covering central tendency and spread, with exact-arithmetic support for int, float, Fraction, and Decimal inputs.

## Public functions

**mean(data)** — Returns the sample arithmetic mean of data; raises `StatisticsError` if data is empty.

**harmonic_mean(data)** — Returns the harmonic mean of data (reciprocal of the mean of reciprocals); appropriate for rates and ratios. Raises `StatisticsError` for empty data or negative values; returns 0 if any value is zero.

**median(data)** — Returns the median of numeric data; for even-length data, interpolates by averaging the two middle values. Raises `StatisticsError` for empty data.

**median_low(data)** — Returns the low median of data; for even-length data, returns the smaller of the two middle values rather than interpolating. Raises `StatisticsError` for empty data.

**median_high(data)** — Returns the high median of data; for even-length data, returns the larger of the two middle values rather than interpolating. Raises `StatisticsError` for empty data.

**median_grouped(data, interval=1)** — Returns the 50th percentile of grouped continuous data using interpolation within the median class interval; `interval` sets the class width. Raises `StatisticsError` for empty data.

**mode(data)** — Returns the single most common value from discrete or nominal data. Raises `StatisticsError` if the data is empty or if there is no unique mode (a tie for most frequent).

**variance(data, xbar=None)** — Returns the sample variance (denominator n−1) of at least two data points; accepts an optional pre-computed mean `xbar` to avoid a second pass. Raises `StatisticsError` for fewer than two data points.

**pvariance(data, mu=None)** — Returns the population variance (denominator n) of at least one data point; accepts an optional pre-computed mean `mu`. Raises `StatisticsError` for empty data.

**stdev(data, xbar=None)** — Returns the sample standard deviation; delegates to `variance` and takes the square root.

**pstdev(data, mu=None)** — Returns the population standard deviation; delegates to `pvariance` and takes the square root.

**StatisticsError** — A `ValueError` subclass raised whenever a function receives data it cannot meaningfully operate on (empty input, insufficient points, no unique mode, or negative values where forbidden).

## How the pieces fit together

The averaging functions (`mean`, `harmonic_mean`) and the spread functions (`variance`, `pvariance`, `stdev`, `pstdev`) all route through the internal `_sum` helper, which accumulates values as exact `Fraction` ratios to avoid floating-point round-off, then converts the final result back to the dominant numeric type of the input; the median family bypasses this and works by sorting and index arithmetic, while `mode` works by frequency counting via `collections.Counter`.

artifact_text:
A numeric statistics library providing measures of central tendency and spread for real-valued data.

**mean** — returns the arithmetic mean (sum divided by count) of the data; raises StatisticsError on empty input.

**harmonic_mean** — returns the harmonic mean (reciprocal of the mean of reciprocals); appropriate for rates and ratios; raises StatisticsError on empty or negative input.

**median** — returns the middle value of sorted data; interpolates the average of the two middle values when the count is even.

**median_low** — returns the middle value for odd-length data, or the lower of the two middle values for even-length data.

**median_high** — returns the middle value for odd-length data, or the upper of the two middle values for even-length data.

**median_grouped** — returns the 50th percentile of grouped continuous data using interpolation within a class interval; accepts an optional `interval` argument (default 1).

**mode** — returns the single most common value; raises StatisticsError if the data is empty or if no unique mode exists.

**variance** — returns the sample variance (sum of squared deviations divided by n−1); accepts an optional precomputed mean `xbar` to avoid recalculating it.

**pvariance** — returns the population variance (sum of squared deviations divided by n); accepts an optional precomputed mean `mu`.

**stdev** — returns the sample standard deviation as the square root of `variance`.

**pstdev** — returns the population standard deviation as the square root of `pvariance`.

The averages and spread functions share a high-precision internal summation layer (`_sum`, `_ss`) that avoids floating-point round-off; all functions preserve the numeric type (int, float, Fraction, Decimal) of the input data.
